name: Black & Whites
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, cream together the sugar and solid white shortening until light and fluffy.
2. Stir in dry milk solids and corn syrup. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. In a separate bowl, sift together the cake flour, baking powder, and salt.
4. Add dry ingredients to the creamed mixture alternately with the water, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Stir in vanilla.
5. Scoop out 1/3 cup of batter and place it on a greased and floured baking sheet. Smooth into a 3-1/2 inch circle.
6. Do not bake more than four cookies on one baking sheet at a time. Bake in a 350°F oven for 18 minutes or until lightly browned.
7. Cool on a rack; frost half of the cookies with chocolate fondant and half with vanilla.
8. Hold the cookie over the bowl while frosting. Work quickly while the fondant is warm.
FONDANT ICING
3/4 cup Water
2 lbs Confectioner's sugar
1/4 cup Light corn syrup
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla extract
2 ounces Sweet chocolate see note
In a large saucepan, combine water, confectioners' sugar, corn syrup, and vanilla. Using a candy thermometer, cook the mixture over medium heat until it reaches 100 degrees.
Note: To make chocolate fondant, melt 2 ounces sweet chocolate and add to 1-1/2 cups of the recipe.
